channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Inside the Interstitium, the Human Body’s Hidden Pathways
item.description: The detection of another circulatory system in the human body could have enormous scientific implications.
item.dc:creator: Avraham Z. Cooper

item.title: How to Win Eurovision With Just a Few Hundred Voters
item.description: Exclusive voting data shows that, despite Eurovision’s assurances, an Israeli campaign could easily have influenced last year’s contest.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall and Mara Hvistendahl

media:content.media:description: Yuval Raphael of Israel topped last year’s public vote and almost won Eurovision. Contest organizers don’t release detailed vote data.

item.title: How Israel Turned Eurovision’s Stage Into a Soft Power Tool
item.description: Israel’s efforts to influence Eurovision’s vote were broader and started years earlier than previously known.
item.dc:creator: Mara Hvistendahl and Alex Marshall
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 11, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Need to Hire an Architect or Designer? Try Dumbo.
item.description: A Brooklyn neighborhood with waterfront views and pricey apartments has become New York City’s latest design hub, with more than 160 architecture and design firms.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu

media:content.media:credit: Brittainy Newman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dumbo is one of the most expensive residential neighborhoods in New York City, but office space rents at a discount compared with Manhattan.

item.title: Airbnb Turns to Black Leaders in Its Bid to Make a Comeback in New York
item.description: The multibillion-dollar corporation is partnering with influential Black pastors as it seeks to regain a foothold in the city’s short-term rental market.
item.dc:creator: Maya King and Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Rev. Al Sharpton favors loosening restrictions on Airbnb rentals in New York.

item.title: Word of the Day: palpable
item.description: This word has appeared in 296 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ¿Qué es el trastorno dismórfico corporal?
item.description: Este padecimiento mental hace que las personas pasen un tiempo excesivo preocupadas por su aspecto, pero va más allá de la vanidad.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

item.title: ‘Off-Campus,’ and 8 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A TV adaptation of a new hockey romance series. And a comedy series starring two “One Tree Hill” alums finds a new home.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: From left: Antonio Cipriano, Stephen Kalyn, Jalen Thomas Brooks and Belmont Cameli in “Off-Campus.”

item.title: Cubans Deserve America’s Generosity, Not Its Cruelty
item.description: If Americans knew what Cuba was enduring, they would demand an end to the U.S. blockade.
item.dc:creator: Pramila Jayapal and Jonathan L. Jackson

item.title: What Middle Powers Fear from the Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Asian nations worry that the president might trade security commitments for better economic terms with China during his planned meeting with Xi Jinping this week.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping after their meeting in Busan, South Korea, in 2025.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Red Card of Black Card? Facing Fate at Thailand’s Draft Lottery.
item.description: Quotation of the day for Monday, May 11 2026.

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3 Finale Recap: All’s Well?
item.description: Valerie makes the case that her tale is one of survival, not humiliation. “You’ve been telling the wrong story,” she tells Jane.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray

media:content.media:description: Despite the tendency for some people around Valerie (Lisa Kudrow) to view her as a doormat, she has always seen every painful moment as a step forward.

item.title: American Passengers Exposed to Hantavirus Are Flying Back to the U.S.
item.description: The passengers will be monitored in Omaha at the country’s only federally funded quarantine center.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao

media:content.media:description: Passengers being evacuated from the MV Hondius on Sunday.

item.title: Best Restaurants in NYC
item.description: The annual list is back. Our new chief critic ranks her favorite places to dine in all five boroughs.
item.dc:creator: Ligaya Mishan

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times

item.title: Thailand’s Thaksin Shinawatra Is Out of Prison. What Will He Do Now?
item.description: Mr. Thaksin has long been a foil to Thailand’s conservative establishment. He still holds significant influence, with his political party a member of the governing coalition.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee and Kittiphum Sringammuang

media:content.media:description: Thaksin Shinawatra, a former prime minister of Thailand, leaving prison in Bangkok on Monday. He was paroled after serving eight months of a one-year sentence.

item.title: A List of 100 Best Restaurants? For These Marathon Eaters, It’s a Dare.
item.description: As rankings proliferate, a cadre of completists have made it their mission to sample every single place on them.
item.dc:creator: Pete Wells

media:content.media:credit: Marissa Alper for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ms. Hunter with the chef Jean-Georges Vongerichten at his Jean-Georges restaurant in New York, which has three Michelin stars. Ms. Hunter tries to eat there once a month.

item.title: The Top Restaurant Trends in New York City for 2026
item.description: Hip crustaceans, dessert trends and the never-ending reservation battle were among the things our chief critic took note of.
item.dc:creator: Ligaya Mishan

item.title: The Best New York City Neighborhoods for Restaurants
item.description: Great meals can be found in virtually any corner of the five boroughs, but here are some areas that stood out to our critic.
item.dc:creator: Ligaya Mishan

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu Say Iran War Is Not Over
item.description: The Trump administration said last week that the war had run its course, but the U.S. president and Israel’s prime minister in interviews on Sunday did not rule out renewed combat.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel and President Trump both suggested in interviews aired on Sunday that there was unfinished business in the conflict with Iran.

item.title: 6 Bodies Found in a Boxcar in Texas, Officials Say
item.description: The bodies were discovered in the city of Laredo. The circumstances of their deaths were not immediately known, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales

media:content.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A freight train passing through Laredo, Texas, in 2025.

item.title: Oil Prices Rise as Prospects for U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Fizzle
item.description: Oil prices rose and stock futures ticked down as investors reacted after the two sides failed to agree on a U.S.-Iran peace deal.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 11, 2026
item.description: Joel Woodford’s second puzzle makes a few exclamations.
item.dc:creator: Elie Levine

item.title: Maya Lin Connects Nature to a New Manhattan Skyscraper and Beyond
item.description: The famed artist and designer drew on her lifelong love of the environment for inspiration for new work in New York and Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Jane L. Levere

media:content.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lin’s new work at the JP Morgan Chase building in New York, a sculpture composed on massive gray stone walls, and the adjoining plaza were envisioned in part as a respite from the cacophony of the city.

item.title: Passengers and Crew Are Evacuated From Ship at Center of Hantavirus Outbreak
item.description: Passengers and crew from a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak were evacuated on Sunday after the vessel anchored off Spain’s Canary Islands.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:description: A passenger from the MV Hondius speaking with medical staff in the Granadilla Port in Tenerife, Canary Islands, on Sunday.

item.title: A One-Party India
item.description: A recent victory in a key state has put Narendra Modi at the helm of a country in which opponents hold almost no power.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi in Kolkata on Saturday.

item.title: Abraham Foxman, Warrior Against Antisemitism, Dies at 86
item.description: For almost three decades, he led the Anti-Defamation League, winning access to presidents and prime ministers and meeting with Pope John Paul II.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Berger

item.title: How Nebraska’s ‘Blue Dot’ Roiled a Democratic House Primary
item.description: A speck of blue on an otherwise red area in some presidential elections, the dot is now dividing candidates in a key midterm contest.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: Nebraska isn’t a swing state, but it can still draw attention in the national conversation in presidential years.

item.title: Hundreds Mourn Eight Members of Lebanese Family Killed in Israeli Strike
item.description: At least eight members of a Lebanese family were killed by an Israeli strike on Saturday. The family was displaced and among those killed was a 6-month-old infant, relatives said.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:description: Mourners paying their respects to the eight family members who were killed in an Israeli strike on southern Lebanon.

item.title: Review: ‘The Receptionist’ Can’t Help You Today
item.description: A stale revival of Adam Bock’s cog-in-the-office-machine dark comedy lacks specificity and bite.
item.dc:creator: Brittani Samuel

media:content.media:description: Katie Finneran, left, and Mallori Johnson in “The Receptionist” at The Pershing Square Signature Center in Manhattan.

item.title: Democratic Angst Could Lead California to Change Primary Rules
item.description: The prospect of Democrats getting shut out of the general election for governor has spurred an effort to eliminate the state’s “top-two” open primary approach.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:description: Voters in California fill out primary election ballots.

item.title: New Books Provide Divergent Views of the Art Market
item.description: Three new books — a sweeping work of nonfiction, a cheeky memoir and a dual biography — provide divergent views on the business of buying and selling, and they are out just in time for New York Art Week.
item.dc:creator: Ted Loos

item.title: Michael Joo Steps Into the Art World’s Spotlight
item.description: Michael Joo is appearing in multiple shows, from New York and the San Francisco Bay Area to a Mediterranean island and Venice.
item.dc:creator: Ted Loos

media:content.media:credit: Oliver Farshi for The New York Times

item.title: A Panorama of Design during the NYCxDesign Festival
item.description: A look at news, products and events during the NYCxDesign Festival.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: Chain mail lighting and a tapestry by Loney Abrams and Johnny Stanish, the husband-and-wife duo and founders of Wretched Flowers.

item.title: A Monument to Chocolate Is Wrapped in Layers of Mexican History
item.description: Combining a colonial house and a contemporary addition, the new Museum of Cacao & Chocolate sits on a grisly Aztec souvenir.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Gallow

media:content.media:credit: Luis Antonio Rojas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Huei, or great Tzompantli. It displays hundreds of human skulls belonging to people who were believed to have been sacrificed in the 15th century.

item.title: Before SoHo, This Building Was at the Heart of New York’s Arts District
item.description: A West Side co-op built for artists and later turned into offices is being reborn as a luxury condominium named Parc Beaufort.
item.dc:creator: Jane Margolies

media:content.media:credit: Stefano Ukmar for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The model one-bedroom apartment at the Parc Beaufort development on West 57th Street.

item.title: With Commissioner Under Pressure, F.D.A. Opens Door to Flavored Vapes
item.description: Though illicit e-cigarettes have flooded in from China, the new policy could allow major tobacco companies to sell from prime shelf space at thousands of stores.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett

media:content.media:credit: Justin J Wee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rates of adolescent vaping have fallen steadily in recent years.

item.title: Trump Says Iran’s Response to Latest U.S. Proposal ‘Totally Unacceptable’
item.description: The United States and Iran have been discussing a 30-day extension to their cease-fire and a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Aaron Boxerman and Adam Sella
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tehran on Saturday. Iran gave its response through Pakistan to an American proposal to end the conflict, Iranian state media reported.

item.title: Trial in 2000 Bombing Case Is Delayed Weeks Before It Was Set to Finally Start
item.description: A Saudi prisoner is accused of orchestrating the bombing of the USS Cole, which killed 17 American sailors in 2000.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Two suicide bombers blew up a bomb-laden skiff alongside the destroyer Cole during a refueling stop off Aden, Yemen, in 2000.

item.title: Here’s the Waffle Recipe You’re Looking for
item.dc:creator: Melissa Clark

media:content.media:credit: Sang An for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Melissa Clark’s waffles.

item.title: Trump Administration Is Considering Pausing the Federal Gas Tax to Lower Prices, Energy Secretary Says
item.description: The tax is a little over 18 cents a gallon for gasoline. With the average national price above $4.50, the measure will provide little relief.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ian C. Bates for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The average national price for a gallon of gasoline was $4.52 on Sunday, according to the AAA motor club.

item.title: Lebanese Mourn Eight Members of One Family Killed in Israeli Strike
item.description: Israel has escalated attacks on Lebanon since Thursday, further unraveling a truce in its war with Hezbollah. Among those killed on Saturday was a 6-month-old infant, relatives said.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir

media:content.media:description: Mourners paying their respects to the eight family members who were killed in an Israeli strike on southern Lebanon.

item.title: Keir Starmer Says No, He Won’t Resign
item.description: But if his opponents mounted a challenge, how would it unfold?
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain speaking to supporters and councilors in London on Friday, the day after elections were held in England, Wales and Scotland.

item.title: After Virginia Redistricting Map Is Tossed, Democrats Search Desperately for a Response
item.description: A conversation involving House members from Virginia and the top House Democrat reflected the fury and desperation that has gripped the party after Friday’s ruling in the state.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:description: Representative Hakeem Jeffries of New York, the minority leader, speaking at the Capitol in Washington last month.

item.title: British Paratroopers Deliver Aid to Remote Islands With Possible Hantavirus Case
item.description: The British military took critical medical supplies to Tristan da Cunha, a tiny British archipelago where authorities have identified a suspected hantavirus case.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg

media:content.media:credit: Ministry of Defense, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A photo released by the British Ministry of Defense showed a parachuter landing on Tristan da Cunha, an archipelago of islands in the South Atlantic Ocean.

item.title: Why You Should Read ‘Yesteryear' Now
item.description: Caro Claire Burke critiques America and asks who, and what, is redeemable?
item.dc:creator: Lauren Jackson

item.title: Chaos Gardening: A Laid-Back Way to Garden
item.description: Welcome to chaos gardening, a laid-back way to turn a patch of ground into a riot of color.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Nazaryan

item.title: Maps: Earthquakes Shake Southern California
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

item.title: How Students Interact With A.I. Is What Matters
item.description: Readers discuss artificial intelligence and writing in the classroom. Also: President Trump’s latest assault on science; election workers.

media:content.media:credit: Lauren Lancaster for The New York Times

item.title: On ‘S.N.L.’, Brett Kavanaugh, Kash Patel and Pete Hegseth Walk Into a Bar
item.description: Matt Damon and Aziz Ansari return to roles they have played on “Saturday Night Live” alongside Colin Jost, in an episode hosted by Damon.
item.dc:creator: Dave Itzkoff
